title: Multiple networks with static IP address
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

My container requires to be configured on multiple networks and for the purpose of testing, I would like to fix those IP addresses to static addresses of a predefined range. `podman run` command works fine with multiple networks by providing a comma-separated list of networks and when using only one network, the static IP address works fine. However, when multiple networks are associated with the container, static IP assignment is rejected by the check:

```
Error: cannot set static IP or MAC address if joining more than one CNI network: invalid argument
```

The issue appears both on rootful and rootless containers.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman network create --driver bridge --subnet 10.84.6.0/24 --gateway 10.84.6.1 net1`

2.  `podman network create --driver bridge --subnet 10.82.0.0/16 --gateway 10.82.0.1 net2`

3. `podman run --network net1,net2 --ip 10.84.6.3 --ip 10.82.0.10`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: cannot set static IP or MAC address if joining more than one CNI network: invalid argument
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

A container that has the associated interfaces with the right static IP address.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

The physical environment on Ubuntu 20.04

comments.body:
This is the plan for podman4.0.

I am closing this issue in favour of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9883
